No Poverty.reason: No direct or indirect evidence in the course syllabus links to poverty eradication or related financial inclusion initiatives.

Zero Hunger.reason: No evidence in the course syllabus, which focuses on game theory in finance, relates to food security or agricultural practices.

Good Health and Well-being.reason: The course content and objectives do not include any topics related to health, well-being, or medical applications.

Quality Education.reason: The course is tertiary education (Target 4.3) and explicitly aims to develop understanding of 'sustainability issues' (Target 4.7) as a core competency.
evidence: 理解與辨別道德、社會責任與永續議題 (Students will understand and be able to identify ethics, social responsibility, and sustainability issues.)

Gender Equality.reason: No specific content or learning outcomes in the syllabus address gender equality or related issues.

Clean Water and Sanitation.reason: The course has no discernible connection to water management, sanitation, or related environmental topics.

Affordable and Clean Energy.reason: There is no evidence in the syllabus linking game theory or finance applications to energy access or renewable energy.

Decent Work and Economic Growth.reason: The course applies game theory to finance, providing skills for financial institutions, supporting Target 8.10 on expanding access to financial services.
evidence: Game Theory With Application in Finance
開課單位：金融三、金融四 Course Department:Money and Banking/B/3&4

Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure.reason: Game theory and problem-solving skills can support strategic innovation and inform access to financial services for enterprises (Target 9.3).
evidence: Game Theory With Application in Finance
問題解決能力 (Students will acquire problem solving skills.)

Reduced Inequalities.reason: Core competency D mentions 'social responsibility'. Game theory can model financial market regulation (Target 10.5), which aims to reduce inequalities.
evidence: 理解與辨別道德、社會責任與永續議題 (Students will understand and be able to identify ethics, social responsibility, and sustainability issues.)

Sustainable Cities and Communities.reason: The course content does not relate to urban planning, infrastructure, or community development.

Responsible Consumption and Production.reason: Core competency D explicitly mentions 'sustainability issues', providing a minimal link to awareness for sustainable development (Target 12.8).

Climate Action.reason: While 'sustainability issues' is mentioned, there's no specific course content linking to climate change mitigation or adaptation.

Life Below Water.reason: No course content or applications relate to marine ecosystems, ocean health, or sustainable fisheries.

Life on Land.reason: The syllabus does not include topics related to terrestrial ecosystems, biodiversity, or land management.

Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions.reason: The course includes 'Principal-agent games', which is fundamental to designing effective, accountable institutions (Target 16.6) and analyzing corruption (Target 16.5).
evidence: 理解與辨別道德、社會責任與永續議題

Partnerships for the Goals.reason: Game theory, as the study of strategic interaction, provides foundational understanding for effective partnerships (Target 17.16) and policy coordination (Target 17.13).
